title: Conversion of Company to LLP Online in India – Fees & Cost
metaDescription: Convert company to LLP online with expert consultant support. Know documents required, conversion fees, cost India, filing service, process & compliance benefits.

hero.headline: Company to LLP Conversion in India – Online Process, Fees & Cost
hero.subtext: Less compliance, more flexibility. Legally seamless LLP conversion by experts.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The conversion of a Private Limited Company into a Limited Liability Partnership (LLP) is regulated by both Companies Act, 2013 and the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008. This conversion allows businesses to combine the operational flexibility of a partnership with the limited liability benefits of a company. It is ideal for entities looking to reduce regulatory compliance, tax burden and enhance operational freedom. Professionals Law offers expert assistance throughout the process from ensuring eligibility and preparing documentation to filing the necessary forms with the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA). Professionals Law ensures a seamless and legally compliant transition to LLP.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: The conversion of a Company into an LLP is often sought to gain relief from complex statutory requirements applicable to private limited companies, such as mandatory audits (if turnover is under ₹40 lakhs), annual filings and board meeting obligations. LLPs offer a easier compliance framework while retaining the features of corporate structure and limited liability protection. Professionals Law provides a structured and legally compliant conversion service to help businesses lower operational overheads, retain tax efficiency and benefit from the flexibility of partnership management under a legal entity structure.

body.title: Reduced Compliance Burden
body.description: LLPs are exempt from several annual compliance norms applicable to companies, including board meetings and statutory audits for small firms.

body.title: No Dividend Distribution Tax (DDT)
body.description: LLPs are not subject to DDT, making profit distribution more tax-efficient compared to private companies.

body.title: Limited Liability Protection
body.description: Just like companies, LLP partners enjoy limited liability, protecting personal assets from business risks.

body.title: Operational Flexibility
body.description: LLPs offer the flexibility of a partnership with a simplified management structure, unlike rigid corporate formalities.

body.title: No Requirement of Minimum Capital
body.description: LLPs have no mandatory minimum paid-up capital requirement, unlike private companies.

criteria: The company must be a Private Limited Company (Public Companies are not eligible).
Must have no security interest in its assets at the time of application.
All shareholders must become partners in the LLP.
The company must be compliant with ROC filings and have no pending dues or litigation.
A valid DSC and DIN of all shareholders is required.
Minimum of 2 partners and 1 designated partner is mandatory.

documents: PAN card and ID proofs of all shareholders/directors
Certificate of Incorporation of the Company
Latest Statement of Accounts certified by a CA (not older than 30 days)
MOA and AOA of the company
Address proof and utility bill of registered office
Digital Signature Certificates (DSCs) of all partners

body.description: Pass a resolution for conversion and authorize partners.
Verify LLP name availability with MCA (usually same as company).
File incorporation and conversion forms with MCA.
Draft and file LLP agreement within 30 days of incorporation.
MCA issues new COI after approval.
Inform ROC about the conversion through Form 14 within 15 days.
Update PAN, GST, bank details and other business licenses.
Delay in Form 14 filing attracts ₹100 per day of default with no upper limit.
Filing incorrect or false information may result in rejection or legal consequences under the LLP Act.
Failure to notify stakeholders may lead to civil liabilities or notices.
Post-conversion non-compliance with LLP rules attracts financial and operational penalties.

body.title: Name Approval & DSC
body.description: 2–3 Working Days

body.title: Filing FiLLiP & Form 18
body.description: 5–7 Working Days

body.title: MCA Processing & COI
body.description: 10–15 Working Days

body.title: Filing LLP Agreement & Form 14
body.description: 5–7 Working Days

body.title: Total Estimated Time
body.description: 20–30 Working Days

body.question: Can any company convert to LLP?
body.answer: Only Private Limited Companies with no secured debt can convert into LLPs.

body.question: Is tax payable on conversion to LLP?
body.answer: If conditions of Section 47(xiiib) of the Income Tax Act are met, the conversion is tax neutral.

body.question: Will a new PAN be required after conversion?
body.answer: Yes, a new PAN will be issued in the LLP’s name.

body.question: Can existing licenses be continued post conversion?
body.answer: Yes, but relevant authorities must be intimated for name and structure change.

body.question: Is audit compulsory in LLP?
body.answer: Only if turnover exceeds ₹40 lakhs or contribution exceeds ₹25 lakhs.

body.question: Can foreign shareholders be part of the conversion?
body.answer: Conversion is allowed if all shareholders agree and comply with FEMA and FDI rules.

body.question: What happens to the existing bank accounts?
body.answer: New bank accounts in the LLP’s name must be opened post-conversion.

body.question: Is DSC required for all shareholders?
body.answer: Yes, all shareholders must have valid DSCs to sign and file documents.

body.question: Does Professionals Law help with post-conversion changes?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law offers full support for updating licenses, bank records and statutory registrations post-conversion.
